prose:
Pyridine most electrophilic site (Fukui f+ ) — DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`, exact four-center integrals) in PySCF via chemkit, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied pyridine.xyz, no geometry optimization (single-point calculation). SCF converged with default PySCF settings (verbose level 4). Mulliken partial charges were used to compute fukui indices. The Mulliken fukui-plus (f+) indices per atom (in input order N, C, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H, H) are:

0.48422572, 0.32447227, 0.14032092, 0.14039588, 0.29962626, 0.29947705, –0.14151320, –0.13368410, –0.13365905, –0.13981630, –0.13984545

and the fukui-minus (f–) indices are:

0.064122671, 0.0061877146, 0.11823139, 0.11826260, 0.11206595, 0.11200985, 0.082848510, 0.098179487, 0.098182260, 0.094960093, 0.094949479

The most electrophilic atom is the nitrogen (atom 1) with f+ = 0.48422572. Live log: fukui_20260704-193849.out (being written to the current working directory). Command run:  
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json --out pyridine_fukui_dft.json  

Result JSON saved as pyridine_fukui_dft.json. No warnings reported.  
integrity.trustworthy = true.
